name: Aunt Ann's Ice Box Rolls
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Ice Water or Mixed with Milk
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the dry yeast in 1/4 cup lukewarm water.
2. Mix all ingredients, including the yeast/water mixture, until a firm dough forms.
3. Place the dough in a greased bowl and cover. Refrigerate overnight.
4. The next day, shape the dough into round walnut-size balls.
5. Place 3 balls in each cup of a greased muffin pan.
6. Let the dough rise until it is higher than the top of the muffin pan.
7. Bake at 350°F for 20 minutes. Optionally, brush tops with butter or margarine.
8. The unbaked dough can be kept in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.
This recipe has been passed down through my MIL's side of the family. Aunt Ann made them first, followed by Aunt Mary Mechling and finally my MIL, Jo Beers.
